summary: Score holds at 3.9/5. Previous fixes landed well — timezone greeting, reduced-motion confetti, dead CSS cleanup all confirmed. But recurring issues persist: the CSS tooltip still only fires on :hover (not :focus-visible), the card-flip back face has no aria-hidden, and DEFAULT_ROSTER + MANAGER_QUOTES dead code in Office.jsx haven't moved. Three new issues found: syncFromGitHub is referenced before definition causing a hoisted useCallback warning in strict mode, the header date badge doesn't use timeZone (fixed in #19 per the greeting but toLocaleDateString still missing it on line 600 — wait, it IS there now, confirmed). The favicon useEffect depends on the full teamVibe object rather than teamVibe.key. Overall the codebase is healthy but the same medium-priority items keep cycling through reviews with no action. Scoring this as 4.0 would require clearing the persistent backlog.

suggestions.title: CSS tooltip only triggers on :hover, not :focus-visible — keyboard users see nothing
suggestions.description: Office.css defines .char[data-tooltip]:hover::after to show task tooltips on the office floor characters. The rule is duplicated (lines ~400 and ~830) but neither variant includes :focus-visible. Since Character components already have tabIndex={0} and onFocus/onBlur handlers, adding .char[data-tooltip]:focus-visible::after { opacity: 1; } would complete the accessibility chain. This has been flagged since review #19 with no fix.

suggestions.title: Card flip back-face not hidden from screen readers when front is showing
suggestions.description: WorkerCard renders both .worker-card__front and .worker-card__back simultaneously. The back face is positioned with CSS (backface-visibility: hidden + rotateY), but screen readers ignore CSS transforms and read both faces. Add aria-hidden={isFlipped ? 'true' : undefined} to the front div and aria-hidden={!isFlipped ? 'true' : undefined} to the back div. Also add role='article' and an aria-label to the outer worker-card div (flagged in #19).

suggestions.title: syncFromGitHub referenced in useEffect before useCallback definition
suggestions.description: App.jsx: the keyboard shortcut useEffect (line ~465) references syncFromGitHub in its dependency array AND calls it directly, but the useCallback defining syncFromGitHub comes later (line ~500). While JavaScript hoisting makes this work at runtime, React's exhaustive-deps lint rule and strict mode may flag this. Additionally the useEffect at line ~465 has [isSyncing, syncFromGitHub] as deps but syncFromGitHub is a useCallback with [] deps (stable ref), so isSyncing alone would suffice. Move the useCallback above the useEffect that depends on it for clarity.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER still in Office.jsx — move to constants.js
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 53-59: DEFAULT_ROSTER lives here while ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS were already extracted to utils/constants.js. This is the 4th consecutive review flagging this. Five-minute fix: move to constants.js as a named export, update the import. Same applies to VIBE_QUOTES and VIBE_WHITEBOARD objects which are pure data.

suggestions.title: MANAGER_QUOTES is permanently unreachable dead code
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 6-15: MANAGER_QUOTES is used as fallback via `(managerVibe && VIBE_QUOTES[managerVibe]) || MANAGER_QUOTES`. But managerVibe always receives a valid key from getTeamVibeKey(), and VIBE_QUOTES has entries for all 5 possible keys, so the fallback never executes. Delete the array. Flagged since review #18.

suggestions.title: Favicon useEffect depends on full teamVibe object instead of teamVibe.key
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~556: the favicon canvas-draw useEffect has [teamVibe] as dependency — the full {label, key} object. While useMemo stabilizes the reference, the intent is fragile. The favicon only uses teamVibe.key for the color lookup. Change dependency to [teamVibe.key]. Similarly the document title useEffect uses [activeWorkers, teamVibe] — using [activeWorkers, teamVibe.key, teamVibe.label] makes intent explicit.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak counter (x3, x4...) has no explanation for new visitors
suggestions.description: The vibe streak number appears next to the vibe pill with no label or tooltip. A first-time visitor seeing 'x7' has no idea what it means. Add a title attribute like 'Consecutive syncs at this vibe level' or wrap it in a small tooltip. Quick win for user comprehension.

suggestions.title: Mobile header overflow — vibe pill + sync button + theme toggle clip at 375px
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport width, the header-right flex container doesn't wrap. The vibe pill, sync button, streak counter, and theme toggle compete for space and overflow. The header has flex-wrap: wrap but header-right does not. Add flex-wrap: wrap to .header-right in the 600px media query, or collapse the vibe pill into the badge on mobile.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons use aria-pressed but should use aria-selected with role='tab'
suggestions.description: The activity filter bar uses role='group' with aria-pressed toggle buttons, but functionally it behaves like a tablist (mutually exclusive selection). Either change to role='tablist' with role='tab' and aria-selected, or keep the current pattern but ensure only one button can be pressed at a time (which it does). The current approach works but is semantically slightly off. Low-effort fix: just swap aria-pressed to aria-current='true' for the active filter.

suggestions.title: Orphaned .stat-card.done CSS with no rendered element
suggestions.description: App.css line 157: .stat-card.done .stat-value { color: var(--blue); } — no 'stat-card done' element exists in StatsBar. Dead CSS from a removed done-count card. Delete or re-add the done stat card.

suggestions.title: Office.jsx CharacterAvatar re-renders on every sync even when worker unchanged
suggestions.description: CharacterAvatar is a pure function of (workerId, role, name, size, emoji, vibeKey) but isn't wrapped in React.memo. Since Office receives the full workers array on every 30s poll, all CharacterAvatar instances re-render even when nothing changed. Wrap CharacterAvatar in React.memo() for a free optimization. Same applies to Character component.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text truncates to 'SH IP IT' on narrow mobile viewports
suggestions.description: The office-whiteboard element is 5.5% wide with word-break: break-word and 0.42rem font. On mobile (375px), the computed width is ~20px, causing 'SHIP IT' to break mid-word. Either increase the whiteboard width in the mobile media query, reduce the text to an emoji-only format on small screens, or hide it entirely below 480px.
